title: VantaDB Campaign Executor — State Machine Definition
description: JSON Schema for a Statewright MachineDefinition, adapted for the VantaDB Campaign Executor. Validates the structure of task execution state machines used in iter.md (C0) and campaign plan files.

id.description: Unique identifier for this state machine (e.g. 'c0-campaign', 'bug-fix', 'code-review').

initial.description: Name of the starting state. Must reference a key in `states`.

context.description: Initial context object passed to every guard evaluation and transition. Shallow-merged with event data on each transition.

_interrupt_return.description: Reserved: stores the return state for the History State pattern ($return target). Set by the runtime when an interrupt fires.

states.description: Map of state name → StateDef. Each state defines its tools, constraints, and transitions.

guards.description: Map of guard name → GuardDef. Guards are declarative predicates evaluated against context.

meta.description: Machine-level metadata for the orchestrator (model hints, danger level, etc.).

interrupts.description: Map of interrupt name → InterruptDef. Interrupts are file-edit-triggered detours using the History State pattern.

StateDef.description: Definition of a single state in the machine. Controls which tools the agent can use, how many iterations it gets, and what transitions are possible.

type.description: 'final' marks a terminal state (execution stops here). 'parallel' enables concurrent sub-states within this state. Omit for normal states.

on.description: Map of event name → TransitionDef. Events are emitted by the agent (e.g. 'DONE', 'FAIL', 'TESTS_PASS').

allowed_tools.description: Whitelist: tools the agent is allowed to use in this state. When set, all other tools are blocked. Prefer over disallowed_tools.

disallowed_tools.description: Blacklist: tools explicitly blocked in this state. When set, all tools EXCEPT these are permitted. Takes precedence if both allowed_tools and disallowed_tools are set.

instructions.description: System instruction injected when entering this state. Tells the agent what to do, what to avoid, and what success looks like.

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of agent iterations (tool-call rounds) allowed in this state before forcing a transition.

safe_next.description: Fallback target state when the model emits an unrecognized transition event. Only fires on unknown events — valid transitions and FAIL are unaffected.

max_edit_lines.description: Maximum total lines of code the agent may edit across all files in this state.

allowed_commands.description: List of allowed shell command prefixes (whitelist for the Bash tool). Only commands starting with any of these prefixes are permitted.

allowed_commands_tool.description: Tool name to enforce command restrictions on. Defaults to 'Bash'.

max_files_per_state.description: Maximum number of distinct files the agent may create or modify in this state.

context_budget_bytes.description: Maximum accumulated context bytes the agent may consume in this state. Prevents context window overflows.

blocked_env.description: Environment variable names blocked from Bash commands in this state. Matching vars are stripped or denied.

env_overrides.description: Environment variable overrides injected as context for this state (advisory, not hard-enforced).

model.description: Recommended model for this state (e.g. 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6'). Clients that support programmatic model switching enforce this; others treat it as advisory.

thinking_level.description: Thinking/reasoning effort level for this state. Clients that support programmatic effort control enforce this; others treat it as advisory.

direct_execution.description: When true, the orchestrating TUI delegates this state to sw-agent via direct execution instead of interactive agent control.

model_ladder.description: Model escalation ladder for direct_execution states. Each entry is a model identifier; the executor escalates when a model fails.

oneOf.title: Simple
oneOf.description: Direct string target — the simplest transition type.

oneOf.title: Full
oneOf.description: Structured transition with optional guard(s) and/or approval gate.

target.description: Target state name. Use '$return' for the History State pattern (returns to _interrupt_return from context).

guard.description: Name of a single GuardDef to evaluate. Transition only proceeds if the guard passes.

guards.description: List of guard names — ALL must pass for the transition to proceed (AND logic).

requires_approval.description: When true, the orchestrator pauses and requests human confirmation before executing this transition.

approval_message.description: Message displayed to the human when requesting approval. Explains what the agent wants to do.

oneOf.title: Guarded
oneOf.description: Array of conditional transitions — first branch whose guard(s) all pass wins. If no branch matches, the transition fails.

oneOf.title: Fork
oneOf.description: Parallel branch execution. Spawns multiple sub-machines, joins when all (or any) complete.

oneOf.title: Invoke
oneOf.description: Delegate to a sub-machine definition. Waits for the sub-machine to complete or fail, then resumes at on_complete or on_fail.

invoke.description: Name/ID of the sub-machine definition to invoke.

on_complete.description: State to transition to when the sub-machine completes successfully.

on_fail.description: State to transition to when the sub-machine fails. Optional — if omitted and sub-machine fails, the parent errors.

input.description: Input data passed to the sub-machine's initial context. Shallow-merged with the sub-machine's defined context.

GuardedTransition.description: A single branch within a Guarded transition array. First matching branch wins.

target.description: Target state if this branch matches.

guard.description: Name of a single GuardDef to evaluate for this branch.

guards.description: List of guard names — ALL must pass for this branch to match (AND logic).

ForkDef.description: Defines a set of parallel branches to execute. Each branch runs its own sub-machine from `initial` to `terminal`. All branches report back before the join completes.

branches.description: Map of branch name → BranchDef. Each branch runs independently.

join.description: Join strategy: 'all' (wait for ALL branches to complete) or 'any' (proceed when ANY branch completes).

on_complete.description: Target state when the join condition is met.

on_fail.description: Target state if any branch fails (for join=all) or all branches fail (for join=any). Optional.

BranchDef.description: A single parallel branch within a Fork. Runs from `initial` state to `terminal` state.

terminal.description: Terminal (final) state for this branch. Branch is complete when this state is reached.

GuardDef.description: A declarative predicate evaluated against the current context. Guards gate transitions — they must pass for the transition to proceed.

field.description: JSON path in the context to evaluate (e.g. 'item_count', 'status', 'payment_method'). Supports dot notation for nested fields.

op.description: Comparison operator. Each operator has specific type requirements for `value`.

value.description:
Value to compare against. Type requirements depend on `op`:
- eq/neq: any JSON value
- gt/gte/lt/lte: number
- in: array of values
- contains: string (substring match)
- exists/not_exists: ignored (value is unused)

InterruptDef.description: An interrupt defines an automatic detour triggered by file edits matching a glob pattern. Uses the History State pattern: the agent is diverted to `target`, and `$return` resumes the prior state.

target.description: State to transition to when the interrupt fires. This state should have a `$return` transition to resume.

InterruptTrigger.description: Condition that fires an interrupt. Currently supports file glob pattern matching.

file_pattern.description: Glob pattern to match against edited file paths. Supports * (non-/ wildcard), ** (any depth), ? (single char).

MachineMeta.description: Machine-level metadata used by the LLM agent layer and orchestrator.

task_type.description: Type of task this machine executes (e.g. 'bug_fix', 'feature_add', 'refactor', 'deploy').

estimated_steps.description: Estimated number of states/steps this machine will visit before reaching a final state.

danger_level.description: Risk level of this machine. 'dangerous' machines may require additional approval gates or restricted tools.

requires_human_approval.description: If true, the entire machine requires human approval before starting execution.

capture_output.description: If true, the orchestrator captures all output (stdout, stderr, files) for logging/audit.

default_model.description: Default model for states that don't specify their own. States with an explicit `model` field override this.
